fields.description:
YOU. BETTER. WITH ALAN.

Alan is building a vertically integrated health partner that unites insurance and smart healthcare delivery into one seamless system. Our vision is to make prevention the new norm of care for all.

Our mission: Help people live in good health to 100 while helping employers feel proud, turning health benefits from a cost centre into their most valuable investment.

By connecting all aspects of care (private, public, and direct to consumer) we create the most member-centric healthcare experience, reducing claims costs while generating new monetization opportunities.

We partner with tens of thousands of companies across France 🇫🇷, Spain 🇪🇸, Belgium 🇧🇪, and Canada 🇨🇦, serving over a million members.


HOW WE WORK: OUR LEADERSHIP PRINCIPLES

 - Mission is the Boss — We think long-term and are methodical optimists who take risks, seeking our mission's success above all else.

 - Distributed Ownership — Accountable enlightened despots: everyone owns their decisions and results.

 - Radical Transparency — All information is accessible and written-first, so everyone can make the best decisions asynchronously.

 - Always Growing — Direct, positive, and caring feedback, combined with self-growth ownership.


MISSIONS

As Alan’s senior Insurance Legal Counsel, you will be a key strategic force across a diverse range of high-stakes legal and business challenges, operating with full autonomy and directly shaping Alan's regulatory positioning, competitive strategy, and growth:

 - You will protect Alan’s competitive edge by defining our strategy on key topics like the angle we take on regulatory requirements that apply to our products and contracts, with direct impact on how we position ourselves in the market. Working from a sharp awareness of the competitive landscape in health and disability insurance, you will set our risk appetite and turn complex regulatory constraints into business advantages.

 - You will be Alan’s legal voice in our most strategic business conversations, directly engaging with key prospects and customers, where your regulatory expertise, sharp posture and negotiation skills will unlock deals. You will own the legal response to major RFPs, define offer strategies for large accounts, and represent Alan directly in complex negotiations.

 - You will maintain our product excellence and the protection of our members, using your expertise and experience to suggest optimal ways to meet regulatory requirements in our products, while improving our members’ experience (e.g., on pricing components, options definition, or UX design within our product, or how we respond to and manage complaints).

 - You will own our insurance compliance posture and ensure our compliance risks are controlled: you will define how regulatory obligations translate into Alan's tools and operational processes, lead the response to unplanned inspections, and ensure our exposure is understood and managed proactively. You will identify strategic risks and report on compliance obligations to Alan's board and committees, with clarity and foresight. You will lead the response to unplanned inspections and digest the results of audits.

 - You will oversee insurance compliance internationally in Alan’s growing markets. Your work will focus on France, yet you will also be accountable for putting in place the right foundational bricks and distributed ownership structure so compliance projects are reflected globally, to ensure a robust set-up in all of Alan’s markets. You will also lead the legal and regulatory dimensions of some of Alan’s new international expansion bets by assisting with the steps to set-up new countries (from regulatory licensing processes, to compliance plan design and implementation, strategic partnership negotiations, and insurance product building).

 - You will develop strategies to influence regulatory evolutions and maintain productive relationships with insurance regulators in Alan’s markets, in close collaboration with regulatory affairs and public affairs teammates.

 - You will increase Alan's legal autonomy by building internal legal frameworks, templates, and decision-making guidelines that reduce reliance on legal counsel. You will share your transversal understanding of the legal stakes for the business with your teammates to increase our overall relevance. You will make judgment calls on legal risk and arbitrate which matters require external support versus in-house handling. You will define and implement an automation roadmap within your scope, and use AI technology widely to generate efficiencies.

You will work as part of the Corporate Unit, led by Marion Bergeret, where teammates from diverse backgrounds combine their skills and intelligence to overcome hurdles and anticipate Alan’s future growth. In your daily work, you will collaborate closely with Elise Toureau and Pauline Sunderwirth, Alan’s insurance legal counsels, as well as with Alan’s Business, Product and Care teams, to find the best pragmatic solutions to the questions raised and elevate the team’s overall capability. As a senior insurance legal expert, you will be expected to raise the bar for how we think about regulatory risk – setting standards, building others’ legal instincts, and giving Alan’s Business and Product teams the confidence to make fast, informed decisions.

At Alan, we embrace that roles evolve and expand beyond their initial scope. Your journey will be filled with exceptional opportunities to grow. You’ll be empowered to tackle new challenges across multiple domains (e.g. new vertical openings, public sector opportunities, regulated product bets, public affairs), building a unique path that matches your ambitions and directly impacts the future of healthcare.


KEY OUTCOMES AFTER SIX MONTHS

 - Owned the legal and regulatory dimension of several high-stakes business opportunities including at least one major RFP response (5,000+ FTEs), a complex contractual negotiation with a key account, or a direct engagement with a significant insurance partner, autonomously and with measurable impact on the outcome.

 - Taken ownership of major legal workstreams from start to finish (e.g., new insurance entity opening and business launch, definition of a company-wide position on a costly or ambiguous compliance requirement, competitiveness assessment of our core insurance products, reorganisation of our global regulatory reporting practices) demonstrating autonomy and velocity in providing sophisticated legal judgment, creativity to overcome obstacles, and high-quality drafting.

 - Provided responsive legal contributions to different internal teams (e.g., Care, Sales, International Expansion) on various legal matters, earning their trust as a reliable legal partner who understands Alan’s goals and provides practical solutions.

 - Built or enhanced legal templates or frameworks that increase Alan’s legal autonomy (e.g., updating and training Sales on objections playbook, new advertising guidelines, or summaries of different international legal schemes).

 - Displayed confidence in our written culture and earned trust as an autonomous voice on insurance legal matters, whose judgment teammates seek out proactively on the most complex issues.


PROFILE WE ARE LOOKING FOR

The senior Insurance Legal & Compliance role requires specific expertise in key areas:

 - Experience:
   
   - At least 10 years of insurance law and/or social protection law experience at a top insurance law firm or an international insurance company.
   
   - At least part of your experience should be commercial, you should have in-depth experience negotiating or litigating complex health & disability insurance deals.
   
   - We also value broad experience across insurance regulatory frameworks, including ACPR guidelines, insurance distribution rules, and legal regulations applicable to health and disability insurance contracts
   
   - Bonus - experience on international markets and drafting in English
   
   - Bonus - experience at regulatory authority would be a plus

 - Languages: Fluency in French and in professional English (written and spoken)

You'll thrive in this role if:

 - Innovative problem-solving: Challenger mindset, creativity, willingness to define innovative solutions and to take risks, to define new practices in line with Alan’s goals

 - Rigor and pragmatism: Attention to detail, focus on excellence combined with simplification

 - Business mind: Pragmatic understanding what really matters/creates value vs. theoretical legal points, and you are comfortable representing Alan’s strong positions with key clients

 - Intellectual curiosity: Curiosity and appetite to become an expert on types of work you are less familiar with

 - Missionary mindset: Passion about working on an ambitious project with highly engaged teammates, hands-on (not about the title)

 - Automation enthusiast (bonus): Interest in leveraging technology and automation tools to streamline legal processes, improve efficiency, and scale legal operations

 - Appetite to grow others: Keen interest in solving problems as a team and helping others grow in their craft, to enable other Alaners to make thoughtful and strong decisions, and to shape future practices across the team.

For this opportunity, we are aiming to hire within the E-F level ranges https://alan.com/en/careers/a/salary-equity.
fields.title: Senior Insurance Legal Counsel
